cwe: CWE-494 - Download of Code Without Integrity Check

threat.title: NPM Package Tampering and Supply Chain Injection
threat.description: Attackers compromise NPM packages through account hijacking, typosquatting, dependency confusion, or malicious maintainer actions to inject malicious code that executes during installation or runtime. This can lead to credential theft, cryptocurrency mining, backdoor installation, or data exfiltration affecting thousands of downstream applications.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker compromises maintainer account via phishing or credential stuffing (no 2FA)
2. Publishes malicious version 2.0.1 of popular package with 50M weekly downloads
3. Malicious postinstall script exfiltrates environment variables to attacker's server
4. Developers run 'npm install' and unknowingly download compromised version
5. Postinstall hook executes, stealing AWS keys, database credentials, API tokens
6. Attacker pivots to cloud infrastructure using stolen credentials
7. Deploys cryptominers, establishes persistence, exfiltrates production data
prerequisites: Package lacks npm provenance attestation and signature verification
Development environment has production credentials in .env files
No package integrity validation (npm audit, Snyk, Socket)
Automated CI/CD pipelines install packages without manual review

cve_references.description: event-stream NPM package version 3.3.6 contained malicious code targeting Copay Bitcoin wallets, stealing private keys and cryptocurrency
ua-parser-js versions 0.7.29, 0.8.0, 1.0.0 were compromised with cryptomining and password-stealing trojans affecting 8M+ weekly downloads
noblox.js-proxy NPM package contained malicious code designed to steal Roblox credentials and game tokens
protobufjs package vulnerable to prototype pollution via malicious .proto files enabling RCE in supply chain attacks

real_world_breaches.impact: 3.9M+ dependent projects compromised; Copay wallet private keys stolen; estimated $100K+ in cryptocurrency theft
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Original maintainer transferred ownership to attacker who added malicious dependency 'flatmap-stream' targeting Bitcoin wallets

real_world_breaches.impact: 8M+ weekly downloads; 3 versions compromised; cryptominer deployed to Linux/Windows; password-stealing trojans installed
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Maintainer's NPM account hijacked via credential theft; attacker published malicious versions 0.7.29, 0.8.0, 1.0.0

real_world_breaches.impact: 21M+ weekly downloads (colors.js), 2.8M+ (faker.js); infinite loop DoS in thousands of applications worldwide
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Maintainer deliberately corrupted packages in protest; versions 1.4.1-1.4.2 (colors), 6.6.6 (faker) caused application crashes

real_world_breaches.impact: 2M+ downloads; NPM credentials stolen from developer machines and sent to attacker
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Maintainer account compromised; version 3.7.2 contained code to steal NPM tokens from ~/.npmrc files

real_world_breaches.impact: 9M+ weekly downloads; password-stealing trojan targeting Windows systems
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Package version 2.0.3 and 2.1.3 compromised with malicious code via account takeover

bug_bounty_reports.summary: NPM dependency confusion attack allowing arbitrary package installation in GitHub Actions

security_research.title: Backstabber's Knife Collection: A Review of Open Source Software Supply Chain Attacks
security_research.key_finding: Analysis of 174 malicious NPM packages shows 56% use typosquatting, 22% dependency confusion, 12% account takeover

security_research.title: 2024 State of the Software Supply Chain Report
security_research.key_finding: 245,000+ malicious NPM packages published in 2024 (156% increase); average attack sophistication increased 742%

mitigations.title: Implement Package Integrity Verification with npm audit and Snyk
mitigations.description: Automatically scan all NPM dependencies for known vulnerabilities and malicious packages using npm audit, Snyk, or Socket Security. Integrate into CI/CD pipelines to block compromised packages before deployment.

code_example.code:
# .github/workflows/security-audit.yml
name: NPM Security Audit

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, develop ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, develop ]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 * * *'  # Daily at midnight

jobs:
  audit:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      
      # NPM Audit (built-in)
      - name: Run npm audit
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=moderate
          if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            echo "Critical vulnerabilities found"
            npm audit fix --dry-run
          fi
      
      # Snyk Security Scan
      - name: Run Snyk to check for vulnerabilities
        uses: snyk/actions/node@master
        env:
          SNYK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SNYK_TOKEN }}
        with:
          args: --severity-threshold=high --fail-on=upgradable
      
      # Socket Security for supply chain analysis
      - name: Socket Security Supply Chain Scan
        uses: SocketDev/socket-security-action@v1
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.SOCKET_TOKEN }}
          fail-on-risk: high
      
      # Generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials)
      - name: Generate SBOM
        run: |
          npm install -g @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm
          cyclonedx-npm --output-file sbom.json
      
      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: sbom
          path: sbom.json

nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms verify software integrity
validation_steps: Run 'npm audit' and verify it completes without critical findings
Test Snyk integration with 'snyk test' locally
Verify CI/CD pipeline fails when high-severity vulnerabilities detected
Confirm SBOM is generated and uploaded to artifact storage
Schedule weekly dependency update PRs with Dependabot/Renovate

mitigations.title: Enable npm Provenance Attestation and Package Signing
mitigations.description: Use npm provenance to cryptographically verify package authenticity and build integrity. Provenance links published packages to source code repository commits, providing tamper-proof verification of package origins.

code_example.code:
# .github/workflows/publish.yml
name: Publish to NPM with Provenance

on:
  release:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write  # Required for provenance
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      
      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test
      
      - name: Build package
        run: npm run build
      
      # Publish with provenance attestation
      - name: Publish to NPM with Provenance
        run: npm publish --provenance --access public
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms verify software integrity
nist_ssdf: PW.4.1 - Verify third-party software integrity using signatures
validation_steps: Configure GitHub Actions with id-token: write permission
Publish package with --provenance flag
Verify provenance on npmjs.com package page
Run verification script to check all dependencies
Configure npm to require provenance for installs

mitigations.title: Enforce Package Lock Files and Subresource Integrity
mitigations.description: Always commit package-lock.json to version control and use 'npm ci' instead of 'npm install' in CI/CD to ensure deterministic, reproducible builds. Enable subresource integrity checking to detect tampering.
iso27001_controls: A.12.5.1 - Installation of software on operational systems
validation_steps: Commit package-lock.json to version control
Replace all 'npm install' with 'npm ci' in CI/CD
Test integrity verification script locally
Verify CI pipeline fails on lockfile mismatch
Configure git hooks to prevent lockfile deletion

mitigations.title: Implement Dependency Firewall and Private Registry
mitigations.description: Use a private NPM registry proxy (Verdaccio, Nexus, Artifactory) to cache vetted packages and block unknown/malicious packages. Implement allowlist-based dependency management.

code_example.code:
# docker-compose.yml - Private NPM Registry with Verdaccio
version: '3.8'

services:
  verdaccio:
    image: verdaccio/verdaccio:5
    container_name: npm-proxy
    ports:
      - "4873:4873"
    volumes:
      - ./verdaccio/config.yaml:/verdaccio/conf/config.yaml
      - ./verdaccio/storage:/verdaccio/storage
      - ./verdaccio/plugins:/verdaccio/plugins
    environment:
      - VERDACCIO_PUBLIC_URL=https://npm.company.com

# verdaccio/config.yaml
storage: /verdaccio/storage/data
plugins: /verdaccio/plugins

# Security settings
security:
  api:
    jwt:
      sign:
        expiresIn: 7d
  web:
    enable: true
    title: Company NPM Registry

# Uplink to public npm (with caching)
uplinks:
  npmjs:
    url: https://registry.npmjs.org/
    cache: true
    timeout: 30s
    maxage: 7d
    max_fails: 3
    fail_timeout: 10m

# Package access control
packages:
  # Internal packages (scoped)
  '@company/*':
    access: $authenticated
    publish: $authenticated
    unpublish: $authenticated
  
  # Allowed external packages (allowlist)
  'express':
    access: $all
    publish: $authenticated
    proxy: npmjs
  
  'react':
    access: $all
    publish: $authenticated
    proxy: npmjs
  
  # Block all other packages by default
  '**':
    access: $authenticated
    publish: $authenticated
    proxy: npmjs

# Authentication
auth:
  htpasswd:
    file: /verdaccio/storage/htpasswd
    max_users: -1

logs:
  - { type: stdout, format: pretty, level: info }

iso27001_controls: A.12.5.1 - Installation of software on operational systems
nist_ssdf: PS.3.1 - Store all forms of code in a repository
validation_steps: Deploy Verdaccio with Docker Compose
Configure client .npmrc to use private registry
Test package caching from public npm
Implement package approval workflow
Monitor registry logs for suspicious activity

mitigations.title: Mandatory 2FA/MFA for NPM Publishing Accounts
mitigations.description: Enforce two-factor authentication for all NPM accounts with publish permissions. Use organization-level settings to require 2FA for all members.

code_example.code:
# Enable 2FA for your NPM account
npm profile enable-2fa auth-and-writes

# Verify 2FA is enabled
npm profile get
# Output should show: tfa: { mode: 'auth-and-writes', pending: false }

# Organization-level 2FA enforcement
# 1. Go to https://www.npmjs.com/settings/[org]/members
# 2. Click "Require two-factor authentication"
# 3. All members must enable 2FA within 7 days or lose publish access

# Generate automation tokens (for CI/CD only)
npm token create --read-only --cidr=0.0.0.0/0
# For publish tokens (use IP restrictions):
npm token create --cidr=52.206.0.0/16  # GitHub Actions IP range

# List and audit all tokens
npm token list

# Revoke compromised tokens immediately
npm token revoke <token_id>

# Add to package.json for developer onboarding
{
  "scripts": {
    "setup:2fa": "echo 'Run: npm profile enable-2fa auth-and-writes'",
    "verify:2fa": "npm profile get | grep -q 'auth-and-writes' && echo '2FA enabled' || echo '2FA not enabled'"
  }
}

nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
nist_ssdf: PO.5.1 - Implement and maintain secure configurations
validation_steps: Enable 2FA on all maintainer accounts
Configure organization to require 2FA
Generate IP-restricted automation tokens
Document token rotation process
Audit token usage monthly

indicators: Unusual package update patterns (version published outside normal working hours)
Packages with obfuscated JavaScript or encoded strings
Packages requesting excessive file system permissions
Dependency versions changing without explicit updates
npm install triggering unexpected CPU/network activity
Environment variables being accessed by postinstall scripts
New package versions published immediately after account login from new IP

log_queries.query:
// Detect suspicious npm package installations
DeviceProcessEvents
| where ProcessCommandLine has "npm install" or ProcessCommandLine has "npm i"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "postinstall" or ProcessCommandLine has "preinstall"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("curl", "wget", "http://", "https://")
| extend SuspiciousActivity = case(
    ProcessCommandLine has "base64", "Base64 Encoding",
    ProcessCommandLine has "eval", "Code Evaluation",
    ProcessCommandLine has "$HOME/.ssh", "SSH Key Access",
    ProcessCommandLine has "$HOME/.aws", "AWS Credentials Access",
    ProcessCommandLine contains ".env", "Environment File Access",
    "Unknown"
  )
| summarize Count = count(), Commands = make_set(ProcessCommandLine) by DeviceName, AccountName, SuspiciousActivity, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where Count > 3
| order by TimeGenerated desc

index=security_logs sourcetype=npm_audit
| where (event_type="package_install" OR event_type="package_update")
| where (package_name IN ("event-stream", "ua-parser-js", "coa", "colors", "faker") AND version IN ("3.3.6", "0.7.29", "0.8.0", "2.0.3", "1.4.1", "6.6.6"))
OR (has_postinstall_script=true AND network_requests_detected=true)
OR (package_age_days < 7 AND download_count < 1000)
| stats count by package_name, version, user, src_ip, install_time
| where count > 5
| sort -count

event.category: "process" AND process.name: ("npm" OR "node") AND process.command_line: (*postinstall* OR *preinstall*) AND (process.command_line: (*curl* OR *wget* OR *fetch*) OR network.direction: "outbound")
AND NOT destination.ip: (10.0.0.0/8 OR 172.16.0.0/12 OR 192.168.0.0/16)

fields @timestamp, @message
| filter @message like /npm (install|i|update)/
| filter @message like /(postinstall|preinstall)/
| filter @message like /(http|https|curl|wget|fetch)/
| stats count() as installation_count by bin(5m) as time_window
| filter installation_count > 10
| sort time_window desc
